description_long: This guard rail consists of a series of thin, irregularly arranged wooden or metal rods, woven together in a crisscross pattern to form a linear barrier. The structure is supported by several vertical posts spaced evenly along its length, each post appearing to be made from the same material as the rails. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with a natural, weathered finish that suggests outdoor use, possibly in parks or along pathways. The ends of the rail are blunt and do not feature any specialized end treatments. There are no visible reflective markings or safety panels attached to the structure. The open, lattice-like design allows visibility and airflow while still serving as a physical barrier.
description: A rustic, crisscross-patterned guard rail made of thin rods and vertical posts, with a weathered, natural finish and no reflective markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length and crisscross pattern of the rails with evenly spaced vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile and the layered arrangement of rods, with minimal depth visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same crisscross pattern and post arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the irregular layering of rods.
